purpose: Open-source task pools for running Aether Programmer Index evaluations outside the SCBE repo.
selection_rule: Prefer task pools with public tasks, reproducible harnesses, pass/fail graders, and artifacts that can be replayed.

strong_coding_assistant_model.base_model: Provides reasoning and code generation, but is not sufficient by itself.
strong_coding_assistant_model.context_system: Fetches the right files, symbols, docs, issues, and prior failures before generation.
strong_coding_assistant_model.task_harness: Defines what done means for each task: tests, build, lint, format, deploy, browser/desktop state, policy compliance.
strong_coding_assistant_model.tool_runtime: Runs shell, git, browser, GitHub, package managers, and optional external agents such as Copilot under bounded permissions.
strong_coding_assistant_model.verification_loop: Reruns tests, records evidence, converts failures to candidate fixes, and tracks trend/recovery.
strong_coding_assistant_model.governance_layer: Keeps model outputs inside tool scope, policy scope, secret rules, and audit receipts.

model_orchestration.principle: Do not make the strongest model do every task. Use the strongest available model as leader, judge, and context router; use cheaper or free models as workers under the same harness.
leader_role: judge whether failure triage is sufficient
model_orchestration.scaling_rule: For 3-1000 agents in flight, model quality is allocated by decision importance, not by task count. Expensive models guard the root and branch points; cheap models do leaf work with verification.
model_orchestration.completion_authority: The harness, tests, policy gate, and evidence packet decide completion. No model is completion authority by itself.

optional_external_tools.use: Can be called as a harness participant for issue-to-PR work, but SCBE remains the verifier and completion authority.
optional_external_tools.expectation: Copilot may propose or patch; SCBE must run build, lint, format, tests, security checks, and evidence scoring before accepting.

optional_external_tools.tool: Sourcegraph/Cody or open-source code search stack
optional_external_tools.use: Can supply codebase search, symbol context, and cross-repo navigation.
optional_external_tools.expectation: Context retrieval improves agent performance only if retrieved evidence is attached to the run packet.

retrieval_stack.role: Fast text search and fallback retrieval.
retrieval_stack.mvp_use: Find terms from issue titles, stack traces, imports, and error messages.
Map names to files before asking a model to inspect code.

retrieval_stack.role: Incremental source parser and syntax tree builder.
retrieval_stack.mvp_use: Extract functions/classes/imports for issue-to-file retrieval and patch impact checks.

retrieval_stack.role: Language-agnostic code intelligence protocol used for precise code navigation.
retrieval_stack.mvp_use: Production path for Sourcegraph-style references, definitions, and cross-repo code graph.
Find similar bugs, policy violations, and security-sensitive code paths.
Use installed language tooling where available instead of parsing everything manually.

lanes.task_pool: Real GitHub issues with repository snapshots and test-based evaluation.
lanes.first_run_plan: Run 1-3 SWE-bench Lite or Verified tasks through an adapter that emits patch, tests, cost, logs, and failure mode.
lanes.completion_rule: Patch applies and benchmark tests pass in the official or equivalent Docker harness.
lanes.why_it_matters: Closest public proxy for real repository repair.

lanes.task_pool: Open-source software-engineering agent and sandbox execution stack.
lanes.first_run_plan: Inspect SWE-agent/SWE-ReX task packaging and reuse compatible sandbox/evidence ideas instead of inventing every wrapper.
lanes.completion_rule: SCBE can launch or emulate an issue-solving task with captured trajectory and replayable execution evidence.
lanes.why_it_matters: Shows the harness shape that makes the same model more useful.

lanes.task_pool: Terminal tasks with instructions, Docker image, tests, and oracle solution.
lanes.first_run_plan: Run a small Docker-backed subset with SCBE CLI/agent-bus as the terminal operator.
lanes.completion_rule: Task tests pass and shell transcript is preserved.
lanes.why_it_matters: Measures the thing chat demos miss: operating the machine.

lanes.task_pool: Exercism-based edit tasks across C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust.
lanes.first_run_plan: Start with the prior Rust failure, then run one Python and one JavaScript task to test language routing.
lanes.completion_rule: Language-specific test suite passes after code edits.
lanes.why_it_matters: Stops us from overfitting to TypeScript/Python comfort zones.

lanes.task_pool: HumanEval+ and MBPP+ function-level tasks with stronger generated tests.
lanes.first_run_plan: Add a cheap local lane for generated function correctness before expensive repo agents.
lanes.why_it_matters: Fast sanity check for code correctness and edge cases.

lanes.task_pool: Curated real Java bugs with triggering tests and human patches.
lanes.first_run_plan: Run one small Java bug-fix task after the multi-language edit harness can run Maven/Gradle-style tests.
lanes.completion_rule: Triggering tests pass and no regression tests fail in the Defects4J harness.
lanes.why_it_matters: Adds Java repair evidence outside Python-heavy SWE-bench lanes.

lanes.task_pool: 1,140 practical code generation tasks with diverse function calls and libraries.
lanes.first_run_plan: Run a tiny instruct split sample to test library-use and function-call reasoning.
lanes.completion_rule: Generated code passes the benchmark evaluator.
lanes.why_it_matters: Measures practical code generation beyond toy HumanEval functions.

lanes.task_pool: Fresh contest-style coding tasks with code generation, repair, execution, and test-output prediction scenarios.
lanes.first_run_plan: Use custom-output evaluation for a small release slice so SCBE can score generated outputs without a custom runner first.
lanes.completion_rule: Submitted JSON outputs pass LiveCodeBench evaluator for selected scenario.
lanes.why_it_matters: Reduces contamination risk and exercises self-repair.

lanes.task_pool: Agent tasks with prompt injection attacks and defenses.
lanes.first_run_plan: Run one tool-use prompt-injection subset against SCBE's gate and record attack success/failure.
lanes.completion_rule: Benign task succeeds while injected instruction fails to control tools or exfiltrate.
lanes.why_it_matters: This is where SCBE should compete: governed tool use, not just better chat.

lanes.task_pool: Real desktop OS tasks across common applications and settings.
lanes.first_run_plan: Treat as later-stage lane after browser/desktop operator stabilizes; start with one low-risk file/config task.
lanes.completion_rule: Programmatic grader confirms final OS state.
lanes.why_it_matters: Measures whether the agent can use the computer, not just write code.

lanes.task_pool: Web tasks across realistic sites with action-based evaluation.
lanes.first_run_plan: Use after the Aether desktop/browser harness can reliably capture screenshots, actions, and receipts.
lanes.completion_rule: Browser task success according to benchmark grader or equivalent state check.
lanes.why_it_matters: Connects coding harness to browser work, deployment, and operator workflows.

lanes.task_pool: Open-source programming exercises and language tracks.
lanes.first_run_plan: Create SCBE-owned mini subsets from public exercises to test weaker models cheaply before full Aider Polyglot.
lanes.completion_rule: Track-specific tests pass and generated diff is minimal.
lanes.why_it_matters: Cheap, understandable tasks for usability testing with weak/free models.

lanes.task_pool: Live public GitHub issues labeled by maintainers.
lanes.first_run_plan: Use gh issue list on selected repositories, create task packets, run agent branch locally, and open PR only after evidence gates pass.
lanes.completion_rule: Maintainer-style PR packet exists with passing local checks, clear diff, and issue closure note.
lanes.why_it_matters: Tests real-world usability and PR workflow beyond fixed benchmarks.
